content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 15, 1990

Kelly Barrales
Treasurer, Friends of Becky Morgan
460 - 2nd Avenue
Redwood City, CA  94063

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-90-131

Dear Ms. Barrales:

     You have requested advice on behalf of Senator Rebecca Morgan
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1

QUESTION

     May Senator Morgan transfer funds received prior to
January 1, 1989, from her "Friends of Becky Morgan" controlled
committee to her controlled committee formed to support her 1992
re-election?

CONCLUSION

     Senator Morgan may transfer any funds currently held in her
"Friends of Becky Morgan" controlled committee to her controlled
committee formed to support her 1992 re-election.

ANALYSIS

     California voters approved Proposition 73 on June 7, 1988.
Proposition 73 amended the Act by establishing limits on
contributions to candidates and their controlled committees.
Proposition 73 also added Section 85306 which states:

_____________
1/  Government Code Section 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Kelly Barrales
Page 2


Any person who possesses campaign funds on the effective date
of this chapter may expend these funds for any lawful purpose
other than to support or oppose a candidacy for elective
office.

On September 14, 1989, the United States District Court for
the Eastern District of California declared Section 85306 valid.
(Service Employees International Union, AFL-CIO, LC, et al. v.
Fair Political Practices Commission, No. CIV. S-89-433 LKK.)
As a result of this ruling, the term "restricted funds" no longer
applies to campaign funds received prior to January 1, 1989, and
such funds may now be used to support or oppose a candidacy for
elective office. However, candidates and officeholders who
possess those funds may not use any campaign funds, including
funds received prior to January 1, 1989, to make contributions to
support or oppose any other candidate for elective office.
(Section 85304.)

All expenditures made and contributions received during a
reporting period must be reported on a committee's campaign
statement. (Section 84211.) Therefore, when reporting a transfer
of funds among a candidate's own controlled committees, the
committee making the transfer of funds should report the
transaction as an expenditure on Schedule E of the Form 490 and
the committee receiving the funds should report it as an increase
to cash on Schedule G of the Form 490.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant
